  

### ohdsilab: Tools for using Roux OHDSI Lab at Northeastern University

------------------------------------------------------------------------

The goals of the ohdsilab R package are two fold:

1.  Streamline working with the OHDSI-Lab database at the Roux Institute
    (and other OMOP CDM databases)

2.  Provide an easier on-ramp for students and researchers new to
    working with the OMOP CDM or SQL databases.

The package contains functions and template code snippets to facilitate
easier use of the OHDSI-Lab. These functions and snippets build on
existing OHDSI R packages like {DatabaseConnector} as well as standard R
packages like {dplyr} and {tidyr}. The package also contains a number of
vingettes intended for R users who are new to OHDSI-Lab, the OMOP CDM,
or working with data in SQL databases from R.

If you’re a new OHDSI-Lab user, after you’ve created your workspace and
have successfully logged in, finish setting up your R environment by
[completing the setup
tutorial](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/01-intro-to-ohdsilab.html)

  

#### An important note on storage

Your OHDSI Lab Workspace is intended to be a resource for *compute*.
***It is NOT intended to be permanent storage***. We do not recommend
saving scripts permanently on your workspace without backing them up
elsewhere.

The best practice is to use a version control system for saving code and
results to a location other than your workspace. We recommend using git
and github for this purpose. Storage on your workspace should be
considered temporary and not a location for long-term storage.

Thankfully, connecting git and github to R is now quite easy. Follow the
steps in [Happy Git with R](https://happygitwithr.com/https-pat) chapter
9 to connect your github account to RStudio. And while using git and
github can be complex, for a single user (you!), it’s as simple as
knowing these four commands:

    git pull
    git add .
    git commit -m "this is a message about the changes you made"
    git push

Information about these commands can be found in [Happy Git with
R](https://happygitwithr.com/git-commands) chapter 21. Or, for a visual
explanation, see the [fantastic illustrations by Allison
Horst](https://allisonhorst.com/git-github).

# Package index

## All functions

- [`dbi_collect()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/dbi_collect.md)
  : Collect using the DBI package
- [`hello()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/hello.md)
  : Hello, World!
- [`icd2omop()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/icd2omop.md)
  : icd2omop
- [`icd2omop2()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/icd2omop2.md)
  : icd2omop2
- [`icd_periods()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/icd_periods.md)
  : Add periods to icd numbers
- [`insertTable_chunk()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/insertTable_chunk.md)
  : Insert a table to your user schema in chunks because speed and bugs
- [`map2omop()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/map2omop.md)
  : map2omop
- [`ohdsilab_connect()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/ohdsilab_connect.md)
  : Connect to the ohdsilab redshift database
- [`omop_join()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/omop_join.md)
  : Join current query to another omop table
- [`pull_concept_set()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/pull_concept_set.md)
  : Get occurrences of of a concept set from ATLAS for a given cohort
- [`rb()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/rb.md) :
  Shortcut to rollback in the case of a redshift error
- [`seedQuery()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/seedQuery.md)
  : Execute a query with a seed for reproducibility
- [`set_seed()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/set_seed.md)
  : Set a seed for reproducibility
- [`str_insert()`](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/reference/str_insert.md)
  : Insert string

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Introduction to ohdsilab (start
  here!)](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/01-intro-to-ohdsilab.md):
- [Getting Started with the OHDSI-Lab
  Box](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/02-getting-started-with-box.md):
- [Generating a
  cohort](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/03-Generating-a-cohort.md):
- [Generating a cohort with
  Capr](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/04-Generating-a-cohort-with_Capr.md):
- [Generating Covariates and Table
  1](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/05-feature-extraction-covariates.md):
- [Generating Characterization
  Files](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/06-feature-extraction-characterization.md):
- [Working with Data from the ohdsilab
  database](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/07-working-with-omop-cdm.md):
- [Visualizing Sample
  Data](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/08-CohortExplorer.md):
- [Patient Level
  Prediction](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/09-PatientLevelPrediction.md):
- [Population Level Estimation using
  CohortMethod](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/10-CohortMethod.md):
- [Population Level Estimation using
  SelfControlledCaseSeries](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/11-SelfControlledCaseSeries.md):
- [Generating a Synthetic
  Dataset](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/12-Eunomia.md):
- [Designing a network analysis using
  Strategus](https://roux-ohdsi.github.io/ohdsilab/articles/13-Stategus.md):
